revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、石人残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]]

revision.comment: テンプレートに所属古墳群情報追加。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]53年（[[1978年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、[[石人]]残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 史跡指定年度は、追加・統合指定以前の大正11年とするのが妥当
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、[[石人]]残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 当古墳は大正11年（1922年）3月8日に国の史跡に指定された。昭和53年（1978年）3月24日付けで、史跡の追加指定・統合指定が行われ、指定名称が「八女古墳群」に変更された。『図説日本の史跡3 原始3』、同朋舎出版、1991、p.206既指定の史跡「[[岩戸山古墳]]」「乗場古墳」「[[石人山古墳]]」「[[善蔵塚古墳]]」「[[弘化谷古墳]]」の5件を統合し、未指定の古墳3基（丸山塚古墳、丸山古墳、茶臼塚古墳）を追加指定したうえで、指定名称を「八女古墳群」とした。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 曖昧さ回避ページへのリンクを付け替え（[[バス]]→[[バス (交通機関)]]）

{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、[[石人]]残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 当古墳は大正11年（1922年）3月8日に国の史跡に指定された。昭和53年（1978年）3月24日付けで、史跡の追加指定・統合指定が行われ、指定名称が「八女古墳群」に変更された。『図説日本の史跡3 原始3』、同朋舎出版、1991、p.206既指定の史跡「[[岩戸山古墳]]」「乗場古墳」「[[石人山古墳]]」「[[善蔵塚古墳]]」「[[弘化谷古墳]]」の5件を統合し、未指定の古墳3基（丸山塚古墳、丸山古墳、茶臼塚古墳）を追加指定したうえで、指定名称を「八女古墳群」とした。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、[[石人]]残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 当古墳は大正11年（1922年）3月8日に国の史跡に指定された。昭和53年（1978年）3月24日付けで、史跡の追加指定・統合指定が行われ、指定名称が「八女古墳群」に変更された。『図説日本の史跡3 原始3』、同朋舎出版、1991、p.206既指定の史跡「[[岩戸山古墳]]」「乗場古墳」「[[石人山古墳]]」「[[善蔵塚古墳]]」「[[弘化谷古墳]]」の5件を統合し、未指定の古墳3基（丸山塚古墳、丸山古墳、茶臼塚古墳）を追加指定したうえで、指定名称を「八女古墳群」とした。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 乗場古墳] - 装飾古墳データベース（[[九州国立博物館]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.comment: [[Special:Diff/84113268/84113332#各地方の史跡一覧記事の改名に伴うリンク付け替え依頼|Bot作業依頼]]: 各地方の史跡一覧記事の改名に伴うリンク付け替え依頼 (九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧) - [[User:Cewbot/log/20190913|log]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Noriba Kofun| 画像=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Noriba Kofun| 画像=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Noriba Kofun| 画像=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Noriba Kofun| 画像=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:new pages]][[category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:New pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[ja:乗場古墳]]

Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound| 名称=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| 所属={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausu-tsuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noriba Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category::dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category::Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * {{Infobox ancient site| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014)| part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| 所在地={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| 規模=Total length: approx. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922年}}| 特記事項=Decorated burial mound| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is designated as a National Historic Site and forms part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, it is speculated that the burial mound may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. Artifacts discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue ware, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was designated a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional burial mounds were integrated into the designation under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously undesignated tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historical Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously designated sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three undesignated tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the burial mound]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == *
