revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| 特記事項=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft}}) {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Noriba Kofun}}

Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| 特記事項=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| 特記事項=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| 特記事項=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft}}) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]] {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| 築造年代=Mid-6th century| 埋葬施設=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| 被葬者=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| 史跡指定=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| 特記事項=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| Construction Period=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft}}) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]] {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.comment: fixing unknown parameters from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built Period=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft}}) [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]] {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built Period=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built Period=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=draft|12=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=draft|12=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan.
