revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:乗場古墳 up to rev 84115271
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、[[石人]]残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 当古墳は大正11年（1922年）3月8日に国の史跡に指定された。昭和53年（1978年）3月24日付けで、史跡の追加指定・統合指定が行われ、指定名称が「八女古墳群」に変更された。『図説日本の史跡3 原始3』、同朋舎出版、1991、p.206既指定の史跡「[[岩戸山古墳]]」「乗場古墳」「[[石人山古墳]]」「[[善蔵塚古墳]]」「[[弘化谷古墳]]」の5件を統合し、未指定の古墳3基（丸山塚古墳、丸山古墳、茶臼塚古墳）を追加指定したうえで、指定名称を「八女古墳群」とした。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 乗場古墳] - 装飾古墳データベース（[[九州国立博物館]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[乗場古墳]] to [[Noriba Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=乗場古墳| 画像=[[ファイル:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
乗場古墳（2014年3月）| 所属=[[八女古墳群]]| 所在地=福岡県八女市吉田字乗場| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=全長約70m| 築造年代=6世紀中頃| 埋葬施設=両袖型横穴式石室| 被葬者=筑紫君葛子？| 史跡指定=[[大正]]11年（[[1922年]]）国の[[史跡]]に指定| 特記事項=装飾古墳}} '''乗場古墳'''（のりばこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[八女市]]吉田に所在する[[前方後円墳]]である。国の[[史跡]]に指定。[[八女古墳群]]を構成する1基。 [[岩戸山古墳]]に近接して築かれていることから、[[筑紫君磐井]]の子「葛子」の墓ではないかと言われている「岩戸山歴史資料館 展示図録」24頁。 == 概要 == 岩戸山古墳から東方約300mに位置する2段築成の前方後円墳で、墳丘長約70m[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba　乗場古墳　岩戸山歴史資料館]、後円部径約30m、高さ約5m、前方部幅約35mで前方部の方がやや低い。[[江戸時代]]の[[久留米藩]]の学者である矢野一貞が著した「筑後将士軍談」の中に「吉田村奈良山」の名で登場し、[[石人]]残欠が存在していたこと、岩戸山古墳同様に周濠・周堤があったことが記されている。 主体部は複室構造の[[横穴式石室]]で、後円部南側のくびれ部寄りに開口部がある。大型石材を用いた奥壁や側壁、玄室の両袖石には赤・黄・青の三色により連続三角文・同心円文・蕨手文が描かれている。保存のため公開はされていない。 石室は明治～大正年間に開口したものと思われ、その間に発見された玉・馬具・須恵器が[[東京国立博物館]]に、金銅装単鳳式環頭大刀柄頭が[[福岡県立福島高等学校]]に所蔵されている。 乗場古墳が造営された時期は、副葬品や装飾文様・石室構造などから6世紀中頃と推定されている。 当古墳は大正11年（1922年）3月8日に国の史跡に指定された。昭和53年（1978年）3月24日付けで、史跡の追加指定・統合指定が行われ、指定名称が「八女古墳群」に変更された。『図説日本の史跡3 原始3』、同朋舎出版、1991、p.206既指定の史跡「[[岩戸山古墳]]」「乗場古墳」「[[石人山古墳]]」「[[善蔵塚古墳]]」「[[弘化谷古墳]]」の5件を統合し、未指定の古墳3基（丸山塚古墳、丸山古墳、茶臼塚古墳）を追加指定したうえで、指定名称を「八女古墳群」とした。 == 交通アクセス == [[ファイル:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|古墳入口]] * [[西日本鉄道|西鉄]]「[[西鉄久留米駅|久留米駅]]」より[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]利用　 * [[JR]][[鹿児島本線]]「[[羽犬塚駅]]」より[[タクシー]]利用 * [[九州自動車道]]「[[広川インターチェンジ (福岡県)|広川インターチェンジ]]」「[[八女インターチェンジ]]」降車 == 脚注 == == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧#福岡県]] * [[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県]] * [[装飾古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 乗場古墳] - 装飾古墳データベース（[[九州国立博物館]]） {{DEFAULTSORT:のりはこふん}} [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[category:福岡県の古墳]] [[category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:八女市の歴史]] [[Category:八女古墳群]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館}}) {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Noriba Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]] {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|12=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|12=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|12=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|qq=draft}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|qq=draft}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|qq=draft}}) {{citation needed|Noriba Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:Noriba Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|前方後円墳|" to "|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame, Fukuoka|qq=}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qq=}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hainuzuka Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kagoshima Main Line|qq=}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Expressway|qq=}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decorated kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu National Museum|qq=}}) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster|lt=Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}| location={{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame, Fukuoka|qq=}} Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|ja|1922nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1922|qq=}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yame, Fukuoka|ja|八女市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|lt=Yame Kofun Cluster|lt=Yame Kofun Group|ja|八女古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Kofun Group|qq=}}. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|ja|西鉄久留米駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishitetsu Kurume Station|qq=}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|ja|羽犬塚駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hainuzuka Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Kagoshima Main Line|ja|鹿児島本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kagoshima Main Line|qq=}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|ja|九州自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Expressway|qq=}}: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|ja|装飾古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Decorated kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|ja|九州国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu National Museum|qq=}}) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]] == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ([[:en:Kyushu National Museum|Kyushu National Museum]]) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]]

These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:福岡県にある国指定の史跡
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]] == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ([[:en:Kyushu National Museum|Kyushu National Museum]]) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yame, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Yame Kofun Group]] [[Category:Decorated kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Noriba Kofun]] [[ja:乗場古墳]] The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * revision.comment: Bot: update category link → National historic sites in Fukuoka prefecture
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]] == External Links == * unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]] == External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ([[:en:Kyushu National Museum|Kyushu National Museum]]) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|乗場古墳|version=84115271|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == *
