revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|qq=}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]] == External Links == * Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=[[File:Noriba-kofun.jpg|250px]]
Noriba Kofun (March 2014) |part_of=[[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]| location=[[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]] Yoshida, Noriba| coordinates={{coordinates|33|13|51.8|N|130|33|26.7|E|}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| }} '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]]. Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}."Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24. == Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum] Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun. The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns. burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=}}. Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure. The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added. == Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car == References == == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and '''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>

'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=File:Noriba-kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.231055555556|N|130.55741666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|qq=|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|qq=|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|qq=|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=File:Noriba-kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.231055555556|N|130.55741666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]

These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car

'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car

'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ([[:en:Kyushu National Museum|Kyushu National Museum]])

'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]

The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]| name=Noriba Kofun| image=File:Noriba-kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.231055555556|N|130.55741666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in [[:en:1922|1922]]| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Yame|Yame, Fukuoka]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the [[:en:Yame Kofun Cluster|Yame Kofun Group]].
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]], while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From [[:en:Nishitetsu Kurume Station|Nishitetsu Kurume Station]]: Accessible by bus * From [[:en:Hainuzuka Station|Hainuzuka Station]] on the [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|JR Kagoshima Main Line]]: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]: Accessible by car
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * [[:en:Decorated kofun|Decorated kofun]]
== External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ([[:en:Kyushu National Museum|Kyushu National Museum]])

The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|WD=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|en|Hainuzuka Station|WD=Q5359747|lt=Hainuzuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=JR Kagoshima Main Line}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}: Accessible by car

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Noriba Kofun]][[ja:乗場古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Noriba Kofun]][[ja:乗場古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Noriba Kofun]][[ja:乗場古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}| name=Noriba Kofun| image=File:Noriba-kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.231055555556|N|130.55741666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame, Fukuoka}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group}}.
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|WD=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|en|Hainuzuka Station|WD=Q5359747|lt=Hainuzuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=JR Kagoshima Main Line}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}: Accessible by car
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|en|Decorated kofun|WD=Q11626716|lt=Decorated kofun}}

The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|WD=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|en|Hainuzuka Station|WD=Q5359747|lt=Hainuzuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=JR Kagoshima Main Line}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Hirokawa Interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway}}: Accessible by car

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Noriba Kofun]][[ja:乗場古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Noriba Kofun]][[ja:乗場古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Noriba Kofun]][[ja:乗場古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}| name=Noriba Kofun| image=File:Noriba-kofun.jpg |part_of={{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}| location={{ill|Yame|WD=Q850492|1=Yame}}| coordinates={{Coord|33.231055555556|N|130.55741666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| Shape=Keyhole-shaped tumulus (前方後円墳)| area=Total length: a11rox. 70 meters| built=Mid-6th century| Burial Facility=Two-sleeve horizontal stone chamber| Interred Persons=Tsunokoso, son of Chikushi Kimi Iwai?| Historic Site Designation=Designated as a National Historic Site in {{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}| Special Notes=Decorated Kofun| | architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}
'''Noriba Kofun''' (乗場古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Yame|en|Yame|WD=Q850492|lt=Yame, Fukuoka|1=Yame}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site and types part of the {{ill|Yame Kofun Cluster|en|Yame Kofun Cluster|WD=Q64615207|lt=Yame Kofun Group|1=Yame Kofun Cluster}}.
Due to its proximity to {{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, it is speculated that the Kofun may be the tomb of Tsunokoso, son of {{ill|Chikushi Kimi Iwai|ja|筑紫君磐井|3=磐井 (古代豪族)|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|2=ja|1=Tsukushi no Iwai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikushi Kimi Iwai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Iwai|qq=}}.<ref>"Exhibition Catalog of the Iwatoyama Historical Museum," p.24.</ref>
== Overview == Located approximately 300 meters east of Iwatoyama Kofun, Noriba Kofun is a two-tiered keyhole-shaped tumulus measuring about 70 meters in total length. The mound features a rear circle approximately 30 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with a front projection approximately 35 meters wide and slightly lower than the rear circle.<ref>[http://museum.iwatoyama.com/archives/field/noriba Noriba Kofun, Iwatoyama Historical Museum]</ref>
Historical records, such as "Chikugo Shōshi Gundan" written by Yano Kazusada during the Edo period, note the presence of stone human fragments, a moat, and a surrounding embankment, similar to Iwatoyama Kofun.
The burial chamber is a multi-chambered horizontal stone chamber with its entrance located near the constricted area on the south side of the rear circle. The walls and stone slabs of the chamber are adorned with patterns in red, yellow, and blue, including repeated triangles, concentric circles, and ferns. The chamber is not open to the public due to preservation concerns.
burial goods discovered between the Meiji and Taishō periods, such as beads, horse equipment, and Sue pottery, are housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}, while a single-phoenix gold-bronze ring-pommeled sword hilt is preserved at {{ill|Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|ja|福岡県立福島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefectural Fukushikan High School|1=Q11592576|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11592576}}.
Noriba Kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-6th century based on burial items, decorative motifs, and chamber structure.
The site was Historic Site1 a National Historic Site on March 8, 1922. On March 24, 1978, additional Kofuns were integrated into the Historic Site under the name "Yame Kofun Group." These included previously unHistoric Site1 tumuli such as {{ill|Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|ja|丸山塚古墳 (八女市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama-tsuka Kofun (Yame)|qq=}} and {{ill|Chausuzuka Kofun|ja|茶臼塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausuzuka Kofun|qq=}}.<ref>『Illustrated Historic Sites of Japan Vol. 3: Prehistoric Part 3』, Dōbōsha Publishing, 1991, p.206.</ref><ref>Five previously Historic Site1 sites—{{ill|Iwatoyama Kofun|ja|岩戸山古墳|1=Iwato-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatoyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato-yama Kofun|qq=}}, Noriba Kofun, {{ill|Sekijinzan Kofun|ja|石人山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekijinzan Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Zenzōzuka Kofun|ja|善蔵塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenzōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Kōkadani Kofun|ja|弘化谷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkadani Kofun|qq=}}—were consolidated, and three unHistoric Site1 tumuli were added.</ref>
== Access == [[File:Noriba-ent.jpg|thumb|200px|Entrance to the Kofun]] * From {{ill|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|en|Nishitetsu Kurume Station|WD=Q7040736|lt=Nishitetsu Kurume Station|1=Nishitetsu Kurume Station}}: Accessible by bus * From {{ill|Hainuzuka Station|en|Hainuzuka Station|WD=Q5359747|lt=Hainuzuka Station|1=Hainuzuka Station}} on the {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|WD=Q1187520|lt=JR Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}: Accessible by taxi * From {{ill|Hirokawa Interchange|ja|広川インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirokawa Interchange|1=Q11485369|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485369}} or {{ill|Yame Interchange|ja|八女インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yame Interchange|1=Yame interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1654393}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Expressway|en|Kyushu Expressway|WD=Q1060803|lt=Kyushu Expressway|1=Kyushu Expressway}}: Accessible by car
== References == <references /> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧#福岡県|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kyushu and Okinawa)|1=list of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}} * {{ill|Decorated kofun|en|Decorated kofun|WD=Q11626716|lt=Decorated kofun|1=Decorated kofun}}
== External Links == * [http://s-kofun.kyuhaku.jp/index.php?g=d_frame_dk_search.php&index=&BACKTOMAP=1&RESULT=0&SEQNO=-&PCD=40&KNO=30&modori=mjl&ACD=7 Noriba Kofun] - Decorated Kofun Database ({{ill|Kyushu National Museum|en|Kyushu National Museum|WD=Q148543|lt=Kyushu National Museum|1=Kyushu National Museum}})
